ops.text: {"config":{"amended_cid":"ALL faithful (snapshot | direct-anchor) proposals must reproduce v001's recorded sentinel prob within 0.001 BEFORE any 002 scoring. buffer_relative proposals are excluded from replay and carried in the ledger flagged recon_method=buffer_relative with null e002 and null v001 self-replay fields. The parallel production fix adds proposals.window_render (the verbatim scored window text); once present it sources replay as a bit-exact snapshot and retires buffer_relative reconstruction.","cell":"amendment","original_cid":"Replaying v001 on its own recorded windows must reproduce the ledger sentinel probs within 0.001 BEFORE any 002 scoring. A proposal whose recorded turn_index does not resolve to its recorded anchor_uuid is buffer_relative (partial live buffer) and its window is not faithfully reconstructable; the CID fails unless every proposal reproduces within tolerance. On failure: halt 002 scoring, emit the per-proposal diff table, and diagnose (render drift / adapter version / tokenizer / window reconstruction).","rationale":"The original CID required all 17 proposals to reproduce v001's recorded sentinel prob within 1e-3; it failed on the 6 buffer_relative proposals. The CID exists to catch render / adapter / tokenizer / window-reconstruction drift, and the bit-exact 11/11 direct-anchor reproduction (diff 0.0) rules all four out. The buffer_relative rows are a data-provenance defect of the recording, not replay drift: the live tailer primes at end-of-file (cc_transcript watch.py:137-142), so an in-flight session's recorded turn_index is buffer-relative and its truncated leading turn cannot be recovered from the transcript.","tolerance":0.001},"config_hash":"2d9fa4ce8b9f","dataset_digest":"30d40130d3f8e226","experiment":"E13-dual-shadow","metrics":{"n_proposals":17},"seed":1729,"verdict":"E13 CID AMENDMENT (faithful subset). Rationale: The original CID required all 17 proposals to reproduce v001's recorded sentinel prob within 1e-3; it failed on the 6 buffer_relative proposals. The CID exists to catch render / adapter / tokenizer / window-reconstruction drift, and the bit-exact 11/11 direct-anchor reproduction (diff 0.0) rules all four out. The buffer_relative rows are a data-provenance defect of the recording, not replay drift: the live tailer primes at end-of-file (cc_transcript watch.py:137-142), so an in-flight session's recorded turn_index is buffer-relative and its truncated leading turn cannot be recovered from the transcript. Amended CID: ALL faithful (snapshot | direct-anchor) proposals must reproduce v001's recorded sentinel prob within 0.001 BEFORE any 002 scoring. buffer_relative proposals are excluded from replay and carried in the ledger flagged recon_method=buffer_relative with null e002 and null v001 self-replay fields. The parallel production fix adds proposals.window_render (the verbatim scored window text); once present it sources replay as a bit-exact snapshot and retires buffer_relative reconstruction."}
